title: Occurrence declarations
analysis:
This task adds occurrence definitions as a new top-level entity kind alongside structures. Occurrences are used for process/transformation modeling and share the same member grammar as structures (params, lets, constraints, subs, ports, guards, minimize/maximize). The key differences are: (1) the keyword is 'occurrence' instead of 'structure', (2) occurrences semantically represent processes with in/out ports for flow composition, (3) chain composition connects output ports to input ports of successive occurrences.

The implementation follows existing patterns closely:
- Grammar: `occurrence_definition` mirrors `structure_definition` exactly (same member rules, same optional `def`/type_params/trait_bounds pattern)
- Parser: `OccurrenceDef` is structurally identical to `StructureDef`, with a `Declaration::Occurrence` variant
- Compiler: The `compile` function already compiles structures via `compile_structure` into `TopologyTemplate`. We add an `EntityKind` field to `TopologyTemplate` (Structure vs Occurrence) and reuse `compile_structure` by refactoring it to accept either entity type. The key addition is a validation warning if an occurrence lacks at least one `in` port and one `out` port.
- Eval: Occurrence instances are created via `sub` declarations just like structure instances. The eval engine already iterates over `module.templates` generically -- no changes needed since occurrences produce TopologyTemplates.

Chain composition (`chain A -> B -> C`) is mentioned in the task tests but is a compile-time desugaring to `connect` statements. This requires new grammar rules, connect statement infrastructure, and port matching logic that are all separate features. The task's core deliverable is the occurrence entity type. Chain/connect syntax is deferred.

Design approach: `OccurrenceDef` in the AST is structurally identical to `StructureDef` to maximize reuse. An `EntityKind` field on `TopologyTemplate` distinguishes them for occurrence-specific validation (port direction warnings). The compile_structure function is refactored to accept both entity types via a shared parameter interface.

prerequisites.description: Add occurrence_definition to tree-sitter grammar.js and regenerate parser.c. The rule mirrors structure_definition but uses 'occurrence' keyword: `occurrence_definition: $ => seq(optional('pub'), 'occurrence', optional('def'), field('name', $.identifier), optional($.type_parameters), optional(seq(':', $.trait_bound_list)), '{', repeat($._member), '}')`. Add to _declaration choice and conflicts if needed. Run `npx tree-sitter generate` in tree-sitter-reify/ to regenerate src/parser.c.

steps.description: Write failing test: parse basic occurrence definition. File: crates/reify-syntax/tests/occurrence_tests.rs. Test `parse_basic_occurrence`: parse `occurrence def Welding { param method : Length }` and expect Declaration::Occurrence(OccurrenceDef) with name='Welding', is_pub=false, 1 Param member named 'method'. This will fail because Declaration::Occurrence and OccurrenceDef don't exist yet.

Add OccurrenceDef struct to reify-syntax/src/lib.rs with same fields as StructureDef (name, is_pub, type_params, trait_bounds, members, span, content_hash). Add Declaration::Occurrence(OccurrenceDef) variant. Add lower_occurrence method to ts_parser.rs mirroring lower_structure (extracts name, is_pub, type_params, trait_bounds, members via the same helpers). Wire into lower_source_file: match 'occurrence_definition' -> call lower_occurrence -> push Declaration::Occurrence. Also add enum collection in first pass for order-independent declarations.

Write test: parse occurrence with in/out ports. Test `parse_occurrence_with_ports`: parse `occurrence def Welding { param method : Length  port workpiece : in StructurePort  port result : out StructurePort }` and verify OccurrenceDef has 3 members (1 Param + 2 Ports), with correct port directions (In, Out) and type names.
Verify port parsing works for occurrences. Since _member and lower_members are shared with structures, ports should parse correctly. Fix any issues if tests fail.

Write test: parse occurrence with trait bounds. Test `parse_occurrence_with_traits`: parse `occurrence def Milling : Machining + CNC { param speed : Length }` and verify OccurrenceDef has trait_bounds=['Machining', 'CNC'], name='Milling', 1 param.
Verify trait bounds parsing works for occurrences via find_trait_bound_list reuse. Fix if needed.

Write test: parse pub occurrence with type parameters and constraints. Test `parse_pub_occurrence_with_type_params`: parse `pub occurrence def Transform<T : Shape> { param scale : Length  constraint scale > 0mm }` and verify is_pub=true, type_params has 'T' with bound 'Shape', members has 1 param and 1 constraint.
Verify pub keyword, type parameters, and constraint members work for occurrences. Should already work via existing helper reuse. Fix if needed.

Write failing test: compile occurrence into TopologyTemplate with EntityKind::Occurrence. File: crates/reify-compiler/tests/occurrence_compile_tests.rs. Test `compile_occurrence_basic`: compile `occurrence def Welding { param method : Length = 10mm }`, verify CompiledModule.templates has 1 template with entity_kind==EntityKind::Occurrence, name='Welding', 1 value cell. This will fail because EntityKind doesn't exist yet.

Add EntityKind enum (Structure, Occurrence) to reify-compiler/src/lib.rs. Add entity_kind field to TopologyTemplate (default: EntityKind::Structure). Refactor compile_structure to take common entity fields and an EntityKind parameter instead of &StructureDef directly -- OR create a thin helper trait/struct that both StructureDef and OccurrenceDef satisfy. In the compile() function, handle Declaration::Occurrence by extracting the same fields (name, is_pub, type_params, trait_bounds, members, span, content_hash) and calling the shared compilation logic with EntityKind::Occurrence. Ensure existing structure compilation sets entity_kind=Structure.

Write test: compile occurrence with ports creates CompiledPort entries. Test `compile_occurrence_with_ports`: compile occurrence with `port workpiece : in StructurePort { param d : Length = 5mm }` and `port result : out StructurePort { param d : Length = 5mm }`, verify template.ports has 2 entries with correct names, directions (In, Out), and member value cells.
Verify port compilation works for occurrences. Since compile_structure logic is reused, ports should compile correctly. Fix any issues.

Write test: compile occurrence with constraints creates ConstraintNodes. Test `compile_occurrence_with_constraints`: compile occurrence with `param speed : Length = 100mm` and `constraint speed > 0mm`, verify template.constraints has 1 entry with Bool result type.
Verify constraint compilation works for occurrences. Should work via shared compile_structure logic. Fix if needed.

Write test: port direction validation warnings for occurrences. Test `compile_occurrence_missing_in_port_warning`: compile occurrence with only an `out` port, verify a warning diagnostic about missing `in` port. Test `compile_occurrence_missing_out_port_warning`: compile occurrence with only an `in` port, verify a warning about missing `out` port. Test `compile_occurrence_with_both_ports_no_warning`: compile occurrence with both in and out ports, verify no port direction warnings.

Add port direction validation for occurrences. After compiling an occurrence template (entity_kind==Occurrence), check ports: if no port has PortDirection::In, emit warning 'occurrence has no input port'. If no port has PortDirection::Out, emit warning 'occurrence has no output port'. Skip this validation for EntityKind::Structure.

Write test: sub declaration referencing an occurrence. Test `compile_occurrence_sub_instantiation`: compile source with occurrence def Welding and structure def Assembly containing `sub step = Welding(method: 10mm)`. Verify the structure template's sub_components has an entry with structure_name='Welding'.

Verify sub declarations can reference occurrence templates. The existing sub compilation stores structure_name as a string. Template lookup by name in the eval engine must find both structure and occurrence templates. Since occurrences now produce TopologyTemplates in the same templates vec, name-based lookup should work. Fix if needed.

Write E2E test: eval occurrence instance. File: crates/reify-eval/tests/occurrence_eval.rs. Parse and compile source: `occurrence def Welding { param speed : Length = 50mm  let double_speed = speed * 2  constraint speed > 0mm }`. Create Engine, call eval(), verify ValueCellId('Welding','speed') has Scalar value 0.05 (50mm in SI), ValueCellId('Welding','double_speed') has Scalar value 0.1.

Verify eval engine processes occurrence templates correctly. The eval loop iterates over module.templates and processes value cells, constraints, and guarded groups identically for all templates. Since occurrence templates are TopologyTemplates, no eval changes should be needed. Fix any issues that arise.

design_decisions.decision: Reuse TopologyTemplate with an EntityKind field rather than creating a separate OccurrenceTemplate
design_decisions.rationale: Occurrences and structures share identical internal structure (value cells, constraints, ports, sub-components, guarded groups). Adding an EntityKind discriminator avoids duplicating all compilation, evaluation, and caching logic. The eval engine can process them uniformly.

design_decisions.decision: OccurrenceDef in the AST is a separate type with identical fields to StructureDef
design_decisions.rationale: A separate Declaration::Occurrence variant makes pattern matching explicit in the compiler and keeps the AST semantically clear (structures vs occurrences are different entity kinds in the language). However, the fields are identical so the same compilation function can process both. A shared trait or destructuring pattern avoids code duplication.

design_decisions.decision: Port direction validation (warning if no in/out ports) only for occurrences, not structures
design_decisions.rationale: Structures represent physical parts and may have any port configuration. Occurrences represent processes/transformations that semantically should have input and output ports for flow composition. The spec states occurrences 'consume input structures and produce output structures'. A warning (not error) allows flexibility while guiding correct usage.

design_decisions.decision: Defer chain composition syntax to a separate task
design_decisions.rationale: Chain syntax (`chain A -> B -> C`) requires new grammar rules (chain_stmt, connect_stmt), desugaring to connect statements, and connect statement implementation with port matching logic. This is a substantial feature beyond the occurrence entity type definition. The occurrence type itself is the prerequisite; chain/connect can be added incrementally.

design_decisions.decision: Grammar uses 'occurrence' keyword with optional 'def', matching structure syntax
design_decisions.rationale: The language spec shows `occurrence def Welding : Joining { ... }`. The grammar for structure already has optional 'def'. Maintaining consistency between entity definition syntaxes keeps the language regular and the parser simple.

design_decisions.decision: Refactor compile_structure to accept entity-kind-agnostic parameters rather than duplicating the function
design_decisions.rationale: compile_structure is ~600 lines of complex logic (scope registration, member compilation, trait conformance, port compilation, content hashing, reference safety). Duplicating it for occurrences would be unmaintainable. Instead, extract the common fields (name, is_pub, type_params, trait_bounds, members, span, content_hash) and pass EntityKind as an additional parameter.

reuse.what: _member grammar rule (param, let, constraint, sub, minimize, maximize, guarded_block, port)
reuse.how: occurrence_definition uses repeat($._member) in its body, identical to structure_definition

reuse.what: lower_members, lower_member, and all member-specific lowering functions (lower_param, lower_let, lower_constraint, lower_sub, lower_port, etc.)
reuse.how: lower_occurrence calls self.lower_members(node) exactly like lower_structure does
lower_occurrence uses these same helpers to extract type parameters, trait bounds, and pub visibility

reuse.what: compile_structure function (scope registration, member compilation, trait conformance, port compilation, content hashing)
reuse.how: Refactor to accept entity-kind-agnostic parameters. Both structures and occurrences call the same function with EntityKind::Structure or EntityKind::Occurrence respectively.

reuse.what: Engine::eval template iteration loop (value cell evaluation, constraint checking, guarded groups)
reuse.how: No changes needed -- eval already iterates over module.templates generically. Occurrence templates are processed identically to structure templates.
Copy the helper function patterns into new occurrence test files for consistent test structure
